parts.text:
You are working in /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe on branch ISS-24/allow-list-connections.

## Task
Fix 3 issues in the integration tests in `cmd/run_integration_test.go`:

### Fix 1: `TestIntegration_AllowedHosts_EmptyAllowlistPermitsPublicHosts`

This test makes a real DNS query to `nonexistent.example.com` which is flaky in air-gapped CI. Change the URL to use `https://192.0.2.1/data` (TEST-NET-1 from RFC 5737 — a public IP that's guaranteed to not be routable but is NOT in our private IP ranges). This avoids DNS dependency. The test should verify that the error is a connection error, NOT an allowlist error.

Find the test and change:
```go
// Before
{ID: "tc_uf3", Name: "url_fetch", Input: map[string]string{"url": "https://nonexistent.example.com/data"}},
```
```go
// After
{ID: "tc_uf3", Name: "url_fetch", Input: map[string]string{"url": "https://192.0.2.1/data"}},
```

### Fix 2: `TestIntegration_AllowedHosts_BlocksLoopbackEvenWithAllowlist`

The assertion uses `||` to accept either error. The behavior is deterministic: `127.0.0.1` fails the allowlist check first. Make the assertion specific:

Find and change:
```go
// Before
if !strings.Contains(body, "not in allowed_hosts") && !strings.Contains(body, "private") {
    t.Errorf("expected allowlist or private IP error, got:\n%s", body)
}
```
```go
// After
if !strings.Contains(body, "not in allowed_hosts") {
    t.Errorf("expected 'not in allowed_hosts' error for raw IP, got:\n%s", body)
}
```

### Fix 3: `TestIntegration_AllowedHosts_BlocksPrivateIP`

Use a more specific assertion substring:

Find and change:
```go
// Before
if !strings.Contains(body, "private") {
    t.Errorf("expected tool result to mention 'private', got request body:\n%s", body)
}
```
```go
// After
if !strings.Contains(body, "private/reserved IP") {
    t.Errorf("expected tool result to mention 'private/reserved IP', got request body:\n%s", body)
}
```

## Verification
Run `cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go test -run "TestIntegration_AllowedHosts" -v ./cmd/` and confirm all 4 tests pass.

Return the test output.
I'll fix the 3 issues in the integration tests. Let me first read the relevant test file to understand the current state.

state.title: TestIntegration_AllowedHosts

parts.text:
All 4 tests pass.

`EmptyAllowlistPermitsPublicHosts` takes 15s because it makes a real TCP connection attempt to `192.0.2.1` (which times out — that's the point), but critically it does **not** fail with an allowlist error, confirming the fix is correct.
